explicit_text: I was born and raised in Chicago, Illinois, living at 6231 Stewart Avenue with my parents before I was drafted. It was there that I finished high school and started studying to be a linotype operator while working at Mercury Typesetting. Chicago was my home base before I entered the Army in April 1943.

explicit_text: I was drafted into the United States Army on April 5th, 1943, choosing it partly because my father had served in World War I. I trained as an infantry replacement at Camp Wheeler, Georgia, and served overseas in the Pacific Theater. The Army was a defining part of my young adult life, shaping my experiences through combat and camaraderie.

explicit_text: I was assigned to the 31st Infantry Division, an Ohio National Guard unit that had been heavily engaged at Guadalcanal. We replaced casualties and trained further on Guadalcanal before moving on to combat at Bougainville. Serving with the 31st was intense and formative, especially during the long fight in the Solomon Islands.

explicit_text: Bougainville was my first combat experience, where we fought the Japanese for thirteen months. The fighting was intense and scary, especially the battle for Hill 700 where we lost several good friends. It was a dramatic and unforgettable part of my service in the Solomon Islands.
